content.full_text:
January 11, 1994


Perry Johnson
Oakland-Piedmont Republican Club
11001 Lochardt Street
Oakland, CA  94605

                                        Re:  Your Request for
                                             Reconsideration of Advice
                                             Our File No. A-93-354a

Dear Mr. Johnson:

     This is in response to your letter dated December 13, 1993,
concerning the campaign filing obligations of the Oakland-Piedmont
Republican Club ("the Club"). Your letter requests reconsideration
of advice provided to you that the Club qualified as a recipient
committee during 1992 and was required to file campaign disclosure
reports. (Advice Letter No. A-93-354.)

     I have reviewed the materials describing the Club's activities
and have determined that the original advice provided to you is
correct. The Club did, in fact, qualify as a recipient committee
during 1992.

     As we discussed, I have explored any possible avenues that
would avoid the need for your group to file 1992/93 reports in
exchange for a promise not to make any future political expenditures.
Unfortunately, the law does not allow such discretion on our part in
these situations. (Government Code § 84400, copy attached.) Therefore,
you must file campaign disclosure reports for 1992 and 1993. If, as
you indicated, the Club will not engage in any reportable activity in
the future, you may terminate the Club's committee status as of the
end of 1993.

     We have enclosed all of the forms you will need to file and
instructions for completing the forms. If you have any further
questions, please contact Lynda Cassady at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660
